nodes.context: Powerful New York street gang led by Paul Kelly, with mostly Italian members but also some Irish and Jewish members.
Rival gang to the Five Points Gang, led by Monk Eastman, predominantly Jewish members.
Small gang formed by Nathan Kaplan after leaving the Five Points Gang, involved in labor slugging.
Small gang formed by Johnny Spanish after splitting from Kaplan's gang, involved in labor slugging.
Labor slugging gang led by Jacob 'Little Augie' Orgen, rival to Kaplan's gang.

edges.context: Kaplan was a former member of the Five Points Gang and organized former members into his own gang.
Johnny Spanish was a former member of the Five Points Gang.
The two gangs were initially allied before a bitter rivalry developed.
A violent war between the two gangs ended with Spanish's murder in 1919.
Kaplan and Orgen fought over labor slugging operations, leading to Kaplan's murder by an Orgen gunman.
